prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains optimization on a 2D loss curve. Combine a moving camera zoom, a tracked point on the graph, a local tangent cue, and a small summary table that updates the training stage story.
completion:
from manim import *

class CameraLossLandscapeTableStory(MovingCameraScene):
    def construct(self):
        def loss_fn(x):
            return 0.12 * (x - 1.1) ** 2 + 0.45 * np.exp(-((x - 1.1) ** 2) / 1.8) + 0.55

        def tangent_line(x_value, width=0.9):
            slope = (loss_fn(x_value + 0.03) - loss_fn(x_value - 0.03)) / 0.06
            center = axes.c2p(x_value, loss_fn(x_value))
            dx = width / (2 * np.sqrt(1 + slope**2))
            dy = slope * dx
            return Line(center + np.array([-dx, -dy, 0]), center + np.array([dx, dy, 0]), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=5)

        title = Text("Optimization as a Descent Story", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[-2, 4, 1], y_range=[0, 2.2, 0.5], x_length=8.4, y_length=4.8, tips=False).shift(DOWN * 0.25)
        graph = axes.plot(loss_fn, x_range=[-1.8, 3.8], color=BLUE_D)
        x_tracker = ValueTracker(3.0)
        probe = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(axes.c2p(x_tracker.get_value(), loss_fn(x_tracker.get_value())), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        guide = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(axes.c2p(x_tracker.get_value(), 0), probe.get_center(), color=GRAY_C, stroke_width=3))
        tangent = always_redraw(lambda: tangent_line(x_tracker.get_value()))
        readout = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("loss", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(loss_fn(x_tracker.get_value()), num_decimal_places=2, font_size=28, color=YELLOW_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.6))
        table = MathTable([["phase", "signal"], ["start", "large slope"], ["middle", "steady drop"], ["end", "small updates"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.42).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.25)
        row_start = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=RED_D, buff=0.05)
        row_mid = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_end = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("Far from the minimum, the local slope points strongly downhill.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("As the point approaches the basin, the camera can zoom to smaller changes.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Near the minimum, updates get smaller and the curve flattens out.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), Create(guide), Create(tangent), FadeIn(readout), Create(row_start), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.65).move_to(probe.get_center()), x_tracker.animate.set_value(1.9), run_time=5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_start, row_mid), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), x_tracker.animate.set_value(1.25), run_time=5)
        self.play(self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.7).move_to(probe.get_center()), x_tracker.animate.set_value(1.05), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_mid, row_end), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), x_tracker.animate.set_value(0.98), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that combines a 3D surface walkthrough with a small fixed-frame dashboard table. Show the full surface first, then highlight two slice curves and a moving probe while the camera orbits.
completion:
from manim import *

class SurfaceSliceDashboardStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        def z_height(u, v):
            return 0.25 * u * np.exp(-(u**2 + v**2) / 8) + 0.45 * np.sin(v) * np.cos(u / 2)

        title = Text("A Surface and Its Informative Slices", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        dashboard = MathTable([["view", "meaning"], ["surface", "all x,y pairs"], ["yellow slice", "vary x at fixed y"], ["red slice", "vary y at fixed x"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.38).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.6)
        caption_a = Text("The surface gives one height for every point in the plane.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Slice curves reduce the surface to simpler one-dimensional stories.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A moving probe makes the slope changes easier to read in 3D.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title, dashboard, caption_a)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_b, caption_c)
        caption_b.set_opacity(0)
        caption_c.set_opacity(0)

        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 2, 1], x_length=7, y_length=7, z_length=4.5)
        surface = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, z_height(u, v)), u_range=[-3, 3], v_range=[-3, 3], resolution=(20, 20), checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D], fill_opacity=0.68)
        x_slice = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(t, 1.2, z_height(t, 1.2)), t_range=[-3, 3], color=YELLOW_D)
        y_slice = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(-0.8, t, z_height(-0.8, t)), t_range=[-3, 3], color=RED_D)
        probe = Dot3D(axes.c2p(-3, 1.2, z_height(-3, 1.2)), color=YELLOW, radius=0.07)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=65 * DEGREES, theta=-55 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(dashboard), Create(axes), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(surface), run_time=5)
        self.play(Create(x_slice), FadeOut(caption_a), FadeIn(caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(y_slice), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), FadeOut(caption_b), FadeIn(caption_c), run_time=2)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.14)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(probe, x_slice), run_time=7, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(3)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that combines stream lines on a plane with a small side profile graph and a compact table of flow regions. The scene should explain the flow in stages rather than showing every annotation at once.
completion:
from manim import *

class StreamlinesProfileTableStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Interpreting a Flow Field with Side Panels", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.32}).shift(LEFT * 2.8 + DOWN * 0.2)
        stream_lines = StreamLines(lambda pos: np.array([1.0 - 0.18 * pos[1], 0.28 * np.sin(pos[0]), 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 0.5], y_range=[-3, 3, 0.5], stroke_width=2.2, max_anchors_per_line=28)
        profile_axes = Axes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[0, 1.6, 0.4], x_length=3.4, y_length=2.4, tips=False).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 1.0)
        profile_graph = profile_axes.plot(lambda y: 1.0 - 0.18 * y, x_range=[-3, 3], color=ORANGE)
        profile_label = Text("horizontal speed vs y", font_size=20, color=ORANGE).next_to(profile_axes, UP, buff=0.15)
        table = MathTable([["band", "behavior"], ["top", "slower"], ["middle", "balanced"], ["bottom", "faster"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 1.65)
        row_top = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_mid = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_bot = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        top_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.4, stroke_color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, 2.0))
        mid_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.4, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, fill_color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, 0.0))
        bot_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.4, stroke_color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, -2.0))
        caption_a = Text("The stream lines show direction, while the side graph summarizes speed.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Different height bands behave differently, so the table names the regions.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=3)
        self.add(stream_lines)
        stream_lines.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.4)
        self.play(Create(profile_axes), Create(profile_graph), FadeIn(profile_label), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(table), Create(top_band), Create(row_top), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(top_band, mid_band), ReplacementTransform(row_top, row_mid), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(mid_band, bot_band), ReplacementTransform(row_mid, row_bot), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.wait(stream_lines.virtual_time / stream_lines.flow_speed)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second ZoomedScene that combines a graph, a tracked probe, and a small table of phases. Zoom into the steep transition region, then pop back out so the viewer sees both the local detail and the global curve.
completion:
from manim import *

class GraphTableZoomedTransitionStory(ZoomedScene):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(zoom_factor=0.38, zoomed_display_height=2.4, zoomed_display_width=4.0, image_frame_stroke_width=10, zoomed_camera_config={"default_frame_stroke_width": 3}, **kwargs)

    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Global Shape and Local Detail", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 10, 1], y_range=[0, 12, 2], x_length=7.2, y_length=4.6, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 1.5 + DOWN * 0.2)
        graph = axes.plot(lambda x: 2.1 + 4.2 * np.tanh((x - 5.0) * 1.4) + 0.08 * x, x_range=[0.6, 9.4], color=BLUE_D)
        tracker = ValueTracker(2.5)
        probe = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(axes.i2gp(tracker.get_value(), graph), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        vline = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), 0), probe.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3))
        table = MathTable([["phase", "curve view"], ["left", "slow drift"], ["middle", "rapid jump"], ["right", "upper plateau"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.36).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.25)
        row_left = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_mid = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_right = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The global curve shows three phases, but the middle jump deserves a closer look.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Zooming into the transition shows how quickly the value changes there.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        frame = self.zoomed_camera.frame
        display = self.zoomed_display
        display.shift(DOWN * 2.1)
        display.display_frame.set_color(RED_D)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), Create(vline), Create(row_left), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(5.0), ReplacementTransform(row_left, row_mid), run_time=4)
        frame.move_to(probe)
        frame.set_color(PURPLE)
        self.play(Create(frame), run_time=2)
        self.activate_zooming()
        self.play(self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation(), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(5.4), run_time=4)
        pop_in = self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation()
        self.play(pop_in, ReplacementTransform(row_mid, row_right), rate_func=lambda t: smooth(1 - t), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(8.2), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)
